full_text_rationale: Full text describes ReasoningBank as a memory framework that distills successful and failed experiences into reusable reasoning strategies, retrieves them for later tasks, and consolidates new experiences back into memory across web and software-engineering benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 006; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Siru Ouyang, Jun Yan, I-Hung Hsu, Yanfei Chen, Ke Jiang, Zifeng Wang, Rujun Han, Long T. Le, Samira Daruki, Xiangru Tang, Vishy Tirumalashetty, George Lee, Mahsan Rofouei, Hangfei Lin, Jiawei Han, Chen-Yu Lee, Tomas Pfister
titulo: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
formato_armazenamento: Reasoning memory items distilled from self-judged successful and failed trajectories, with structured fields such as title, description, and when-to-apply guidance; new experiences are consolidated back into the repository.
metricas_desempenho: Resolve rate, average interaction steps, benchmark success metrics, ablation scores
baseline_comparado: No Memory, Synapse, AWM and success-only/failure-trajectory ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows as successful and failed experiences are distilled and consolidated after tasks; MaTTS scales interaction experience to synthesize higher-quality memory.
resultado_principal: ReasoningBank improves across web and software-engineering benchmarks. On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash improves from 34.2% No Memory to 38.8%, and Gemini-2.5-pro improves from 54.0% to 57.4%, with fewer average interaction steps.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash ReasoningBank reports 38.8% resolve versus 34.2% No Memory and 35.4% Synapse; Gemini-2.5-pro reports 57.4% versus 54.0% No Memory and 53.4% Synapse. Failure-trajectory ablations report larger gains than success-only memory variants.
custo_vs_baseline: Reports efficiency as fewer interaction steps: average 2.8 fewer steps than No Memory and 1.3 fewer than other memory baselines on SWE-bench Verified; no monetary cost extracted.
limitacoes: Memory extraction and success/failure labeling rely on LLM judgments; software-engineering evidence centers on SWE-bench Verified; monetary cost and detailed memory-size scaling are not extracted here.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark mix, LLM-as-judge reliability, transfer from web to software-engineering tasks, model-family dependence, and reproduction of comparator memory mechanisms may affect validity.
